rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Query co-expression partners of a candidate HOG across species

Source: R/comparison.R
get_coexpressed_hogs.Rd

For a given HOG, finds which other HOGs co-express with it in each species network, then aggregates across species. Useful after identify_module_hubs and classify_hub_conservation to explore the co-expression neighborhood of a hub gene.

get_coexpressed_hogs(
  candidate_hog,
  networks,
  orthologs,
  species = names(networks),
  species_trait = NULL,
  min_species = 2L,
  edges = NULL
)

Arguments

candidate_hog

Character string: the HOG ID to query (e.g., "HOG42").

networks

Named list of compute_network outputs, keyed by species abbreviation.

orthologs

Data frame with columns Species1, Species2, hog (from parse_orthologs or extract_orthologs).

species

Character vector of species to query (default: all in networks).

species_trait

Optional named character vector mapping species to trait labels (e.g., c(SP_A = "annual", SP_B = "perennial")). Enables the coexpressed_traits output column.

min_species

Minimum number of species where co-expression must occur for a partner to be reported (default 2).

edges

Optional stacked edge data frame from run_pairwise_comparisons. When provided, attaches conservation statistics for each partner HOG.

Value

A data frame with columns:

partner_hog

HOG ID of the co-expression partner

n_species

Number of species where co-expression occurs

coexpressed_species

Comma-separated species names

coexpressed_traits

Comma-separated unique trait values of those species (sorted); NA if species_trait is NULL

mean_weight

Mean co-expression weight across species

When edges is provided, three additional columns are appended:

partner_conserved

Logical: does the partner HOG have any "conserved" edge?

partner_mean_effect

Mean effect size from edges for the partner HOG

partner_min_q

Minimum q-value from edges for the partner HOG

Rows are sorted by n_species (descending), then mean_weight (descending).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
trait <- c(SP_A = "annual", SP_B = "annual",
           SP_C = "perennial", SP_D = "perennial")
partners <- get_coexpressed_hogs("HOG42", networks, orthologs,
                                  species_trait = trait,
                                  min_species = 2L, edges = edges)
# Annual-only partners
partners[partners$coexpressed_traits == "annual", ]
} # }
